questions.q: What is a vector? Provide an example.
questions.answer: A vector is a quantity with both magnitude and direction. For example, displacement of 6 km from north to south is a vector.

questions.q: What is the difference between a scalar and a vector?
questions.answer: A scalar is a quantity with magnitude only, whereas a vector is a quantity with both magnitude and direction.
A unit vector is a vector with a magnitude of 1.
A zero vector is a vector with a magnitude of 0.
The position vector of a point is a vector from the origin to that point.

questions.q: What is the magnitude of the vector 2i + 3j?
questions.answer: The magnitude of the vector 2i + 3j is √(2² + 3²) = √13.

questions.q: Find the resultant vector of the vectors 2i + 3j and i - 2j.
If A = i + j and B = 2i - 3j, find 2A - 3B.
Find the vector equation of the line joining the points (1, -1, 0) and (0, 1, 2).
questions.answer: Let P(1, -1, 0) and Q(0, 1, 2). Then the position vector of P is i - j and the position vector of Q is -j + 2k. The required equation is <i - j, -j + 2k> = λ<i - j, -j + 2k>.

questions.q: Find the vector equation of the plane passing through the points (1, 2, -1), (2, -1, 2), and (-1, 3, 1).
questions.answer: Let A(1, 2, -1), B(2, -1, 2), and C(-1, 3, 1). Then the position vectors of A, B, and C are i + 2j - k, 2i - j + 2k, and -i + 3j + k respectively. The required equation is (i + 2j - k).(2i - j + 2k) × (-i + 3j + k) = 0.

questions.q: Find the angle between the vectors 2i + 3j and i - 2j.
questions.answer: The angle between the vectors 2i + 3j and i - 2j is cos⁻¹((2 × 1 + 3 × (-2))/(√2² + 3² × √1² + (-2)²)) = cos⁻¹(-11/5√13).

questions.q: Find the projection of the vector 2i + 3j on the vector i - 2j.
questions.answer: The projection of the vector 2i + 3j on the vector i - 2j is ((2 × 1 + 3 × (-2))/(√2² + 3²)) × (i - 2j) = (-11/5)(i - 2j).

questions.q: Find the distance between the points (1, 2, -1) and (2, -1, 2).
